member.summary: Marker interface to explicitly declare the identity type of an aggregate. This is especially useful for aggregates that use strong typed identifiers (e.g., Vogen value objects wrapping Guid/string) to enable automatic identity inference in command handlers.

member.name: T:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.IGeneratedAsyncEvolver`2

member.summary: Interface for source-generated evolvers that handle all-sync conventional methods with ShouldDelete. Used for self-aggregating types.

member.summary: Interface for source-generated evolvers that handle all-sync conventional methods without ShouldDelete. Used for self-aggregating types.

member.summary: Small strategy to set the identity of an entity

member.name: T:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.IRefersToAggregate
member.summary: Marker interface for attributes that refer to an aggregate type. When applied to attributes on method parameters, the source generator will discover and generate evolvers for the parameter's aggregate type, ensuring Marten and Polecat can use source-generated projections even without explicit Snapshot<T>() registration.

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase`4.FilterIncomingEventsOnStreamType
member.summary: When used as an asynchronous projection, this opts into only taking in events from streams explicitly marked as being the aggregate type for this projection. Only use this if you are explicitly marking streams with the aggregate type on StartStream()

member.summary: Hook that you can override in order to do "event enrichment" where you might batch up database lookups to add information to events prior to applying them in a projection running asynchronously. Note that this method is called *after* slicing, but before applying events

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase`4.DetermineAction(`0,`1,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{JasperFx.Events.IEvent})
member.summary:
Override this method to apply workflow mechanics to your aggregate with a purely synchronous method that does not require any additional data lookup.
It is valid to override this method or ApplyAsync, but not both!

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase`4.DetermineActionAsync(`3,`0,`1,JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.IIdentitySetter{`0,`1},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{JasperFx.Events.IEvent},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Override this method if your projection requires complex workflow like deleting or "un-deleting" projected documents *and* you require asynchronous data access code as part of the workflow.

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase`4.EvolveAsync(`0,`1,`3,JasperFx.Events.IEvent,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Override this method to write explicit logic for this aggregation to evolve or create a snapshot based on a single event at a time. Returning null when the document previously existed will cause the document to be deleted.
member.param: The current snapshot, if it exists
The aggregate identifier
The current query session
The event to apply
Cancellation token
member.returns: The evolved snapshot, a new snapshot, or null. Returning null when the document previously existed deletes the document, returning null when no document existed does nothing.

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Aggregation.JasperFxAggregationProjectionBase`4.Evolve(`0,`1,JasperFx.Events.IEvent)
member.summary: Override this method to write explicit logic for this aggregation to evolve or create a snapshot based on a single event at a time using only synchronous code. Returning null when the document previously existed will cause the document to be deleted.
member.param: The current snapshot, if it exists
The aggregate identifier
The event to apply
member.returns: The evolved snapshot, a new snapshot, or null. Returning null when the document previously existed deletes the document, returning null when no document existed does nothing.

member.summary: The projection daemon is disabled in this application and will not be started as part of the application

member.name: F:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.DaemonMode.Solo
member.summary: The system will start up the complete projection daemon with the assumption that this node is the only execution node. This is appropriate for single node deployments and local development usage

member.name: F:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.DaemonMode.HotCold
member.summary: Marten will ensure that the full async projection daemon will only execute on one node at a time, with fail over to other nodes.

member.name: P:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.IReadOnlyDaemonSettings.StaleSequenceThreshold
member.summary: If the projection daemon detects a "stale" event sequence that is probably cause by sequence numbers being reserved, but never committed, this is the threshold to say "just look for the highest contiguous sequence number newer than X amount of time" to trigger the daemon to continue advancing. The default is 3 seconds.

member.name: P:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.IReadOnlyDaemonSettings.SlowPollingTime
member.summary: Polling time between looking for a new high water sequence mark if the daemon detects low activity. The default is 1 second.

member.name: P:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.IReadOnlyDaemonSettings.FastPollingTime
member.summary: Polling time between looking for a new high water sequence mark if the daemon detects high activity. The default is 250ms

member.name: P:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.IReadOnlyDaemonSettings.HealthCheckPollingTime
member.summary: Polling time for the running projection daemon to determine the health of its activities and try to restart anything that is not currently running

member.name: P:JasperFx.Events.Daemon.IReadOnlyDaemonSettings.AsyncMode
member.summary: Projection Daemon mode. The default is Disabled

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Grouping.SliceGroup`2.EventStep`2.ForEntityId``1(System.Func{`3,``0})

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Grouping.SliceGroup`2.EventStep`2.ForEntityIdFromEvent``1(System.Func{JasperFx.Events.IEvent{`3},``0})
member.summary: Specify *how* the enrichment can find the identity TId from the events of type TEvent for entities of type TEntity

Execute a batched enrichment operation for events of type , allowing the caller to perform a single lookup using any external query mechanism captured in the provided delegate and apply the results directly to the active slices.
member.remarks:
This method is intended for enrichment scenarios where related entities cannot be resolved by a simple identity lookup, or where applying the enrichment requires custom logic beyond a basic reference or replacement.
The delegate receives all slices and all matching events in the current batch, so it can: query data in one round trip, match the results to events using any correlation key, and then mutate the slices by referencing entities, replacing events, or adding synthetic events.
An upstream aggregate cache, if available, is passed through to the delegate but is never accessed or mutated by this method. Cache usage is entirely controlled by the caller.
member.param:
A delegate invoked once per enrichment execution. The delegate receives:
The active instances for this batch
The complete set of events of type occurring in those slices
An optional upstream aggregate cache for entities that the caller may consult or populate
A cancellation token
A cancellation token used to cancel the enrichment operation.

member.name: M:JasperFx.Events.Projections.AsyncOptions.SubscribeAsInlineToAsync
member.summary:
Use this option to prevent having to rebuild a projection when you are simply changing the projection lifecycle from "Inline" to "Async" but are making no other changes that would force a rebuild
Direct that this projection had previously been running with an "Inline" lifecycle now run as "Async". This will cause the system to first check if there is any previous async progress, and if not, start the projection from the highest event sequence for the system.
